You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Food. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

Predicate: country - Example triple: (Ajoblanco | country | Spain)
Predicate: ingredient - Example triple: (Ajoblanco | ingredient | Almond)
Predicate: region - Example triple: (Ajoblanco | region | Andalusia)
Predicate: division - Example triple: (Almond | division | Flowering plant)
Predicate: family - Example triple: (Almond | family | Rosaceae)
Predicate: order - Example triple: (Almond | order | Rosales)
Predicate: course - Example triple: (Amatriciana sauce | course | Italian meal structure)
Predicate: dishVariation - Example triple: (Amatriciana sauce | dishVariation | "Onion, garlic, black pepper, chili")
Predicate: mainIngredient - Example triple: (Amatriciana sauce | mainIngredient | "Tomatoes, guanciale, cheese, olive oil")
Predicate: leader - Example triple: (Andalusia | leader | Susana Díaz)
Predicate: alternativeName - Example triple: (Asam pedas | alternativeName | "Asam padeh")
Predicate: creatorOfDish - Example triple: (Asam pedas | creatorOfDish | Malay cuisine)
Predicate: creator - Example triple: (Bakewell pudding | creator | "Rutland Arms, Bakewell, in 1820")
Predicate: servingTemperature - Example triple: (Bakewell pudding | servingTemperature | "Warm (freshly baked) or cold")
Predicate: carbohydrate - Example triple: (Barny Cakes | carbohydrate | 18.0 g)
Predicate: fat - Example triple: (Barny Cakes | fat | 4.8 g)
Predicate: year - Example triple: (Barny Cakes | year | 1999)
Predicate: related - Example triple: (Bhajji | related | Pakora)
Predicate: similarDish - Example triple: (Bhajji | similarDish | "Pakora and other fritters made from wheat or corn flour")
Predicate: genus - Example triple: (Celery | genus | Apium)
Predicate: language - Example triple: (China | language | Standard Chinese)
Predicate: isPartOf - Example triple: (Derbyshire Dales | isPartOf | Derbyshire)
Predicate: currency - Example triple: (France | currency | Euro)
Predicate: demonym - Example triple: (India | demonym | Indian people)
Predicate: capital - Example triple: (Indonesia | capital | Jakarta)
Predicate: ethnicGroup - Example triple: (Java | ethnicGroup | Javanese people)
Predicate: foundationPlace - Example triple: (Mondelez International | foundationPlace | Chicago)
Predicate: officialLanguage - Example triple: (Philippines | officialLanguage | Philippine English)
Predicate: leaderTitle - Example triple: (United States | leaderTitle | President of the United States)
Predicate: served - Example triple: (Bakewell pudding | served | "Warm  or cold")
Predicate: protein - Example triple: (Barny Cakes | protein | 1.8 g)
Predicate: class - Example triple: (Chicken | class | Bird)
Predicate: servingSize - Example triple: (Barny Cakes | servingSize | 30.0 g)

```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of food‑related triples into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    clauses = []
    for t in triples:
        s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object
        # Basic phrasing for each predicate
        if p == "country":
            clauses.append(f"{s} is from {o}")
        elif p == "ingredient":
            clauses.append(f"{s} contains {o}")
        elif p == "region":
            clauses.append(f"{s} originates from {o}")
        elif p == "division":
            clauses.append(f"{s} belongs to the division {o}")
        elif p == "family":
            clauses.append(f"{s} is in the family {o}")
        elif p == "order":
            clauses.append(f"{s} is classified under the order {o}")
        elif p == "course":
            clauses.append(f"{s} is served as a {o}")
        elif p == "dishVariation":
            clauses.append(f"{s} has variations such as {o}")
        elif p == "mainIngredient":
            clauses.append(f"The main ingredients of {s} are {o}")
        elif p == "leader":
            clauses.append(f"The leader of {s} is {o}")
        elif p == "alternativeName":
            clauses.append(f"{s} is also known as {o}")
        elif p == "creatorOfDish" or p == "creator":
            clauses.append(f"{s} was created by {o}")
        elif p == "servingTemperature" or p == "served":
            clauses.append(f"{s} is served {o}")
        elif p == "carbohydrate":
            clauses.append(f"{s} contains {o} of carbohydrates")
        elif p == "fat":
            clauses.append(f"{s} contains {o} of fat")
        elif p == "protein":
            clauses.append(f"{s} contains {o} of protein")
        elif p == "year":
            clauses.append(f"{s} was introduced in {o}")
        elif p == "related":
            clauses.append(f"{s} is related to {o}")
        elif p == "similarDish":
            clauses.append(f"{s} is similar to {o}")
        elif p == "genus":
            clauses.append(f"{s} belongs to the genus {o}")
        elif p == "language":
            clauses.append(f"The language of {s} is {o}")
        elif p == "isPartOf":
            clauses.append(f"{s} is part of {o}")
        elif p == "currency":
            clauses.append(f"The currency of {s} is {o}")
        elif p == "demonym":
            clauses.append(f"The demonym for {s} is {o}")
        elif p == "capital":
            clauses.append(f"The capital of {s} is {o}")
        elif p == "ethnicGroup":
            clauses.append(f"The ethnic group of {s} is {o}")
        elif p == "foundationPlace":
            clauses.append(f"{s} was founded in {o}")
        elif p == "officialLanguage":
            clauses.append(f"The official language of {s} is {o}")
        elif p == "leaderTitle":
            clauses.append(f"The leader title of {s} is {o}")
        elif p == "servingSize":
            clauses.append(f"The serving size of {s} is {o}")
        else:
            # Fallback generic phrasing
            clauses.append(f"{s} {p} {o}")

    # Assemble a single sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import List, Dict

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: List[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of food‑related triples into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""
    
    # Group triples by subject for better sentence flow
    subject_triples: Dict[str, List[Triple]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_triples:
            subject_triples[t.subject] = []
        subject_triples[t.subject].append(t)
    
    def format_triple(t: Triple) -> str:
        s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object
        if p == "country":
            return f"{s} is from {o}"
        elif p == "ingredient":
            return f"{s} contains {o}"
        elif p == "region":
            return f"{s} originates from {o}"
        elif p == "division":
            return f"{s} belongs to the division {o}"
        elif p == "family":
            return f"{s} is in the family {o}"
        elif p == "order":
            return f"{s} is classified under the order {o}"
        elif p == "course":
            return f"{s} is served as a {o}"
        elif p == "dishVariation":
            return f"{s} has variations such as {o}"
        elif p == "mainIngredient":
            return f"The main ingredients of {s} are {o}"
        elif p == "leader":
            return f"The leader of {s} is {o}"
        elif p == "alternativeName":
            return f"{s} is also known as {o}"
        elif p == "creatorOfDish" or p == "creator":
            return f"{s} was created by {o}"
        elif p == "servingTemperature" or p == "served":
            return f"{s} is served {o}"
        elif p == "carbohydrate":
            return f"{s} contains {o} of carbohydrates"
        elif p == "fat":
            return f"{s} contains {o} of fat"
        elif p == "protein":
            return f"{s} contains {o} of protein"
        elif p == "year":
            return f"{s} was introduced in {o}"
        elif p == "related":
            return f"{s} is related to {o}"
        elif p == "similarDish":
            return f"{s} is similar to {o}"
        elif p == "genus":
            return f"{s} belongs to the genus {o}"
        elif p == "language":
            return f"The language of {s} is {o}"
        elif p == "isPartOf":
            return f"{s} is part of {o}"
        elif p == "currency":
            return f"The currency of {s} is {o}"
        elif p == "demonym":
            return f"The demonym for {s} is {o}"
        elif p == "capital":
            return f"The capital of {s} is {o}"
        elif p == "ethnicGroup":
            return f"The ethnic group of {s} is {o}"
        elif p == "foundationPlace":
            return f"{s} was founded in {o}"
        elif p == "officialLanguage":
            return f"The official language of {s} is {o}"
        elif p == "leaderTitle":
            return f"The leader title of {s} is {o}"
        elif p == "servingSize":
            return f"The serving size of {s} is {o}"
        else:
            return f"{s} {p} {o}"
    
    # Build clauses for each subject
    all_clauses = []
    for subject, t_list in subject_triples.items():
        if len(t_list) == 1:
            all_clauses.append(format_triple(t_list[0]))
        else:
            # Multiple triples for same subject - create complex sentence
            first_clause = format_triple(t_list[0])
            rest_clauses = [format_triple(t) for t in t_list[1:]]
            combined = first_clause
            if rest_clauses:
                combined += ", " + ", ".join(rest_clauses[:-1])
                if len(rest_clauses) > 1:
                    combined += ", and " + rest_clauses[-1]
                else:
                    combined += " and " + rest_clauses[0]
            all_clauses.append(combined)
    
    # Assemble into final sentence
    if len(all_clauses) == 1:
        return all_clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        return ", ".join(all_clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + all_clauses[-1] + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
>>>>>>> REPLACE
